Header file for a configurable status bar layer, including structures, enums, and functions for rendering and managing status bar properties.

# Purpose
The code defines a C header file for a configurable status bar layer, intended for use in a user interface. It provides a set of constants, data structures, and functions to manage and render a status bar that can display time, loading status, or custom text. The status bar is designed to be adaptable across different platforms, with specific height configurations for each platform type. The file includes definitions for the status bar's modes, such as `StatusBarLayerModeClock`, `StatusBarLayerModeLoading`, and `StatusBarLayerModeCustomText`, as well as separator modes like `StatusBarLayerSeparatorModeNone` and `StatusBarLayerSeparatorModeDotted`.

The header file also defines the `StatusBarLayer` and `StatusBarLayerConfig` structures, which store configuration details such as text buffers, colors, and separator settings. Functions are provided to create, initialize, render, and destroy status bar layers, as well as to set and get properties like colors, title text, and info text. The status bar can display progress information and has a timeout feature for reverting to clock mode. The file is intended to be included in other C source files to provide a reusable component for managing status bars in applications.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/app_timer.h`
- `applib/ui/animation.h`
- `applib/graphics/gtypes.h`
- `layer.h`
- `applib/event_service_client.h`


# Data Structures

---
### StatusBarLayerMode
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``StatusBarLayerModeClock``: Default mode where time display takes priority.
    - ``StatusBarLayerModeLoading``: Indicates that something is loading and may or may not revert automatically.
    - ``StatusBarLayerModeCustomText``: Displays custom text with an optional auto-revert to the default mode.
- **Description**: Defines the different modes for a status bar layer, including a default clock mode, a loading indicator mode, and a custom text mode with optional auto-revert functionality.


---
### StatusBarLayerSeparatorMode
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``StatusBarLayerSeparatorModeNone``: No separator will be shown.
    - ``StatusBarLayerSeparatorModeDotted``: A dotted separator at the bottom of the status bar.
- **Description**: Defines the modes for displaying a separator in a status bar, with options for no separator or a dotted separator at the bottom.


---
### StatusBarLayerSeparator
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `mode`: Holds the mode of the status bar separator, defined by `StatusBarLayerSeparatorMode`.
- **Description**: Defines a structure for a status bar separator with a mode that determines its appearance, such as whether it is dotted or not. The structure is designed to accommodate future animation-specific variables related to the separator.


---
### StatusBarLayerConfig
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `title_text_buffer`: Stores the center title text with a fixed buffer size.
    - `info_text_buffer`: Stores the right info text with a fixed buffer size.
    - `foreground_color`: Specifies the color of the text and other foreground elements.
    - `background_color`: Specifies the color of the background.
    - `separator`: Defines the separator mode for the status bar.
    - `mode`: Defines the mode of the status bar, such as clock or loading.
- **Description**: Configures a status bar layer with text buffers for title and info, colors for foreground and background, a separator mode, and a status bar mode. It allows customization of the appearance and behavior of a status bar, including text display and visual separation from other content.


---
### StatusBarLayer
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``layer``: A `Layer` object that represents the graphical layer of the status bar.
    - ``config``: A `StatusBarLayerConfig` object that holds configuration settings for the status bar.
    - ``title_timer_id``: A pointer to an `AppTimer` used to manage the timer for reverting the title.
    - ``tick_event``: An `EventServiceInfo` object that manages time update events.
    - ``previous_min_of_day``: An integer that stores the previous minute of the day for time tracking.
- **Description**: Represents a configurable status bar layer that includes a graphical layer, configuration settings, a timer for title management, and event handling for time updates. It is designed to be used within a user interface to display status information such as time, loading status, or custom text.

---
### status\_bar\_layer\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L144>)

Creates and initializes a new status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new `StatusBarLayer` on the heap with default settings. The function initializes the layer with default text and background colors, and sets its frame to cover the full width of the screen with a fixed height. The status bar is marked dirty after creation, indicating it needs to be redrawn. If the function cannot allocate memory for the layer, it returns `NULL`. Ensure to destroy the layer with `status_bar_layer_destroy` when it is no longer needed to free resources.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the newly created `StatusBarLayer`, or `NULL` if allocation fails.
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_create`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_create>)  (Implementation)


---
### status\_bar\_layer\_destroy<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L148>)

Destroys a previously created status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to release resources associated with a `StatusBarLayer` that was created using `status_bar_layer_create`. This function must be called to prevent memory leaks when the status bar layer is no longer needed. It is safe to pass a null pointer to this function, in which case it will do nothing.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to the `StatusBarLayer` to destroy. Must be a valid pointer returned by `status_bar_layer_create` or null. The function does nothing if this parameter is null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_destroy`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_destroy>)  (Implementation)


---
### status\_bar\_layer\_get\_layer<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_get_layer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L160>)

Retrieves the root layer of a status bar.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the root `Layer` of a `StatusBarLayer`, which serves as the parent for its sub-layers. This is useful when you need to manipulate or interact with the status bar as a `Layer` object, such as adding it to a parent layer or setting its frame. Ensure that the `StatusBarLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` from which to retrieve the root `Layer`. Must not be null. If null, the behavior is undefined.
- **Output**: A pointer to the `Layer` that represents the root of the given `StatusBarLayer`.
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_get_layer`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_get_layer>)  (Implementation)

- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the current background color of a `StatusBarLayer`. This is useful when you need to know the existing color configuration of the status bar for display or logic purposes. Ensure that the `status_bar_layer` parameter is a valid pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` object before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` from which to get the background color. Must not be null. If the pointer is invalid, the behavior is undefined.
- **Output**: Returns the `GColor` representing the background color of the specified `StatusBarLayer`.
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_get_background_color`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_get_background_color>)  (Implementation)

- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` from which to get the foreground color. Must not be null. If null, the behavior is undefined.

### status\_bar\_layer\_set\_title<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_set_title}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L184>)

Sets the title text of a status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to set the title text of a `StatusBarLayer`. The function copies the provided text into an internal buffer for display. If the `revert` parameter is true, the title will automatically revert to displaying the clock after a predefined timeout. The `animated` parameter determines if the status bar should animate when the title is set. Ensure that the `StatusBarLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to the `StatusBarLayer` whose title text you want to set. Must not be null.
    - `text`: A pointer to a null-terminated string containing the text to set as the title. The function copies this text into an internal buffer.
    - `revert`: A boolean value indicating whether the title should revert to the clock display after a timeout. If true, the title will revert.
    - `animated`: A boolean value indicating whether the status bar should animate when setting the title. If true, the animation will occur.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_set_title`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_set_title>)  (Implementation)


---
### status\_bar\_layer\_get\_title<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_get_title}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L190>)

Retrieves the current title text from a status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the current title text from a `StatusBarLayer`. This function is useful when you need to access or display the title text that is currently set in the status bar. Ensure that the `status_bar_layer` parameter is not null before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` from which to retrieve the title text. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the `StatusBarLayer`.
- **Output**: A pointer to a character string containing the current title text of the specified `StatusBarLayer`. The returned string is managed internally and should not be modified or freed by the caller.
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_get_title`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_get_title>)  (Implementation)


---
### status\_bar\_layer\_reset\_title<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_reset_title}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L194>)

Resets the title text to display the clock.
- **Description**: Use this function to change the title text of a `StatusBarLayer` to show the current time. This function is useful when you want to revert any custom title text back to the default clock display. Ensure that the `cb_data` parameter points to a valid `StatusBarLayer` object. The function does not return a value and does not handle invalid input, so the caller must ensure the validity of the `cb_data`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `cb_data`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` object. Must not be null and must point to a valid `StatusBarLayer` instance. The function does not perform any checks on this parameter, so invalid input can lead to undefined behavior.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_reset_title`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_reset_title>)  (Implementation)

### status\_bar\_layer\_set\_info\_text<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_set_info_text}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L199>)

Sets the info text in the status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the information text displayed in the status bar layer. This function copies the provided text into an internal buffer, which is limited to 8 characters. The status bar layer must be initialized before calling this function. After setting the text, the status bar layer is marked as dirty, prompting a redraw.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function will assert if this parameter is null.
    - `text`: A pointer to a null-terminated string containing the text to display. The text is copied into an internal buffer with a maximum size of 8 characters. If the text is longer, it will be truncated.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_set_info_text`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_set_info_text>)  (Implementation)


---
### status\_bar\_layer\_set\_info\_progress<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_set_info_progress}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L207>)

Displays progress information on a status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to set the progress information on a `StatusBarLayer`, which can be useful for indicating pagination or completion status. The function displays the progress as a fraction if the total is 99 or less, and as a percentage if the total is greater than 99. Ensure that the `current` value does not exceed the `total` value, as the function will not display anything in that case. This function must be called with a valid `StatusBarLayer` pointer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` structure. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `current`: The current progress value. Must be less than or equal to `total`.
    - `total`: The total value representing 100% progress. Determines whether progress is displayed as a fraction or percentage.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_set_info_progress`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_set_info_progress>)  (Implementation)


---

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L213>)

This function is useful when you need to access or verify the info text that is currently set. Ensure that the `status_bar_layer` is not null before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` from which to retrieve the info text. Must not be null.
- **Output**: A pointer to a character string containing the current info text of the specified `StatusBarLayer`. The string is null-terminated.
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_get_info_text`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_get_info_text>)  (Implementation)

### status\_bar\_layer\_reset\_info<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_bar_layer_reset_info}} -->

Clears the info text in the status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to clear the information text displayed in the status bar layer. This function is useful when you want to reset the info section to an empty state. It must be called with a valid `StatusBarLayer` pointer. After clearing the text, the status bar layer is marked as dirty, which triggers a redraw.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function will assert if this parameter is invalid.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`status_bar_layer_reset_info`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_reset_info>)  (Implementation)

Retrieves the current separator mode of a status bar layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the current separator mode of a given `StatusBarLayer`. This is useful when you need to check how the status bar visually separates itself from other content. Ensure that the `status_bar_layer` parameter is not null before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` from which to get the separator mode. Must not be null. If null, the behavior is undefined.
- **Output**: Returns the current `StatusBarLayerSeparatorMode` of the specified status bar layer.

### status\_layer\_get\_title\_text\_width<!-- {{#callable_declaration:status_layer_get_title_text_width}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h#L233>)

Calculates the width of the title text in the status bar.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine the width of the title text currently displayed in a `StatusBarLayer` when it is in clock mode. This function is useful for layout calculations where the width of the text is needed to adjust other UI elements. The function assumes that the `StatusBarLayer` is in `StatusBarLayerModeClock` mode and will assert if it is not. Ensure that the `StatusBarLayer` is properly initialized and in the correct mode before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `status_bar_layer`: A pointer to a `StatusBarLayer` structure. The `StatusBarLayer` must be in `StatusBarLayerModeClock` mode. The caller retains ownership and must ensure it is not null.
- **Output**: Returns the width of the title text as an `int16_t`. The width is calculated based on the current text format and layout settings of the `StatusBarLayer`.
- **See Also**: [`status_layer_get_title_text_width`](<status_bar_layer.c.md#status_layer_get_title_text_width>)  (Implementation)
